content.content:
1	//! Phase-B reconcile shim: re-exports the canonical `LuaState` from
2	//! `lua-vm` and provides an extension trait holding every method the
3	//! Phase-A stdlib translation used to call on the Phase-A stub.
4	//!
5	//! TODO_ARCH(phase-b-reconcile): all extension-trait method bodies are
6	//! `todo!("phase-b-reconcile: <name>")`. They must move to real
7	//! implementations on `lua_vm::state::LuaState` itself; that work lives in
8	//! `lua-vm`, not here. The shim exists only so stdlib code keeps compiling
9	//! while the canonical `LuaState` API stabilises.
10	//!
11	//! Where a trait method's name collides with an inherent method on the
12	//! canonical `LuaState`, Rust resolves to the inherent method. Most
13	//! Phase-A call sites compile through the inherent method unchanged; the
14	//! handful that depend on a different return shape (e.g. `state.push(...)?`
15	//! against the canonical `pub fn push(&mut self, val: LuaValue)`) are
16	//! patched at the call site.
17	
18	#![allow(dead_code, unused_variables, clippy::too_many_arguments)]
19	
20	use lua_types::{
21	    arith::ArithOp,
22	    closure::{LuaCFnPtr, LuaClosure},
23	    error::LuaError,
24	    gc::GcRef,
25	    string::LuaString,
26	    userdata::LuaUserData,
27	    value::{LuaThread, LuaValue},
28	    LuaType,
29	    LuaStatus,
30	};
31	
32	pub use lua_vm::state::LuaState;
33	
34	/// Bare function callable from Lua. C: `lua_CFunction`.
35	#[allow(non_camel_case_types)]
36	pub type lua_CFunction = fn(&mut LuaState) -> Result<usize, LuaError>;
37	
38	/// Pseudo-index for the `i`-th upvalue of a C function.
39	/// C: `#define lua_upvalueindex(i)`
40	pub fn upvalue_index(i: i32) -> i32 {
41	    -1_001_000 - i
42	}
43	
44	/// Comparison operations (eq, lt, le). C: `LUA_OP*`.
45	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
46	pub enum CompareOp {
47	    Eq,
48	    Lt,
49	    Le,
50	}
51	
52	/// Reader-callback type for `lua_load`. C: `lua_Reader`.
53	pub type LuaReader<'a> = dyn FnMut() -> Option<Vec<u8>> + 'a;
54	
55	/// Writer-callback type for `lua_dump`. C: `lua_Writer`.
56	pub type LuaWriter<'a> = dyn FnMut(&[u8]) -> Result<(), LuaError> + 'a;
57	
58	/// Debug introspection record. C: `lua_Debug`.
59	#[derive(Debug, Default, Clone)]
60	pub struct LuaDebug {
61	    pub name: Option<Vec<u8>>,
62	    pub namewhat: Vec<u8>,
63	    pub what: u8,
64	    pub source: Vec<u8>,
65	    pub short_src: Vec<u8>,
66	    pub linedefined: i32,
67	    pub lastlinedefined: i32,
68	    pub currentline: i32,
69	    pub nups: u8,
70	    pub nparams: u8,
71	    pub isvararg: bool,
72	    pub istailcall: bool,
73	    pub ftransfer: u16,
74	    pub ntransfer: u16,
75	}
76	
77	impl LuaDebug {
78	    pub fn name_bytes(&self) -> &[u8] { self.name.as_deref().unwrap_or(b"?") }
79	    pub fn namewhat_bytes(&self) -> &[u8] { &self.namewhat }
80	    pub fn what_bytes(&self) -> &[u8] { match self.what { b'L' => b"Lua", b'C' => b"C", b'm' => b"main", _ => b"?" } }
81	    pub fn short_src_bytes(&self) -> &[u8] { &self.short_src }
82	    pub fn source_bytes(&self) -> &[u8] { &self.source }
83	}
84	
85	/// Extension trait wiring every Phase-A stub method onto the canonical
86	/// `LuaState`. Bodies are `todo!("phase-b-reconcile: …")`. When the
87	/// canonical type already defines a method with the same name, Rust's
88	/// inherent-first resolution makes this trait method unreachable (the
89	/// inherent one wins) — the trait is then only providing the *missing*
90	/// methods. Conflicting call-sites whose shape no longer matches the
91	/// inherent signature are patched in their respective stdlib modules.
92	pub trait LuaStateStubExt {
93	    fn push_value(&mut self, idx: i32) -> Result<(), LuaError> { todo!("phase-b-reconcile: push_value") }
94	    fn push_copy(&mut self, idx: i32) -> Result<(), LuaError> { todo!("phase-b-reconcile: push_copy") }
95	    fn push_string(&mut self, s: &[u8]) -> Result<(), LuaError> { todo!("phase-b-reconcile: push_string") }
96	    fn push_bytes(&mut self, s: &[u8]) -> Result<(), LuaError> { todo!("phase-b-reconcile: push_bytes") }
97	    fn push_fstring(&mut self, args: std::fmt::Arguments<'_>) -> Result<(), LuaError> { todo!("phase-b-reconcile: push_fstring") }
98	    fn push_c_function(&mut self, f: lua_CFunction) -> Result<(), LuaError> { todo!("phase-b-reconcile: push_c_function") }
99	    fn push_c_closure(&mut self, f: lua_CFunction, n: i32) -> Result<(), LuaError> { todo!("phase-b-reconcile: push_c_closure") }
100	    fn push_where(&mut self, level: i32) -> Result<(), LuaError> { todo!("phase-b-reconcile: push_where") }

crates/lua-vm/src/vm.rs
crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs
crates/lua-vm/src/debug.rs
crates/lua-vm/src/object.rs
No matches found

930	        Err(e) => {
931	            state.push(e.into_value());
932	            false
933	        }
934	    };
935	    // C: return finishpcall(L, status, 2);
936	    finish_pcall(state, ok, 2)
937	}
938	
939	// ── tostring ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
940	
941	/// Converts any value to its string representation (calls `__tostring` if
942	/// present).
943	///
944	/// C: `static int luaB_tostring(lua_State *L)`
945	pub(crate) fn tostring_fn(state: &mut LuaState) -> Result<usize, LuaError> {
946	    // C: luaL_checkany(L, 1); luaL_tolstring(L, 1, NULL);
947	    state.check_arg_any(1)?;
948	    // to_display_string pushes the converted string and returns a handle to it.
949	    // TODO(port): to_display_string method needs implementing on LuaState.
950	    state.to_display_string(1)?;
951	    Ok(1)
952	}
953	
954	// ── Registration table ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
955	
956	/// All base-library functions registered into the global table by `open`.
957	///
958	/// C: `static const luaL_Reg base_funcs[]`
959	///
960	/// PORT NOTE: The C table includes placeholder entries
961	/// `{LUA_GNAME, NULL}` and `{"_VERSION", NULL}` that `luaopen_base` fills in
962	/// separately.  Those are omitted here; `open()` sets them explicitly.
963	pub(crate) const BASE_FUNCS: &[(&[u8], LuaLibFn)] = &[
964	    (b"assert",         assert_fn),
965	    (b"collectgarbage", collectgarbage_fn),
966	    (b"dofile",         dofile_fn),
967	    (b"error",          error_fn),
968	    (b"getmetatable",   getmetatable_fn),
969	    (b"ipairs",         ipairs_fn),

310	}
311	
312	// ── luaT_objtypename ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
313	
314	// C: const char *luaT_objtypename (lua_State *L, const TValue *o)
315	/// Return the human-readable type name for a Lua value.
316	///
317	/// For tables and full userdata whose metatable defines `__name` as a string,
318	/// that string is returned.  Otherwise falls back to the standard `type_name()`
319	/// for the base type.
320	///
321	/// PORT NOTE: C returns `const char*` (static or LuaString bytes with stable
322	/// lifetime).  Rust returns `Vec<u8>` to avoid lifetime entanglement between
323	/// the static `TYPE_NAMES` entries and GcRef'd `LuaString` bytes.
324	/// PERF(port): Vec allocation on every call — profile in Phase B; may become
325	/// a `Cow<'static, [u8]>` once lifetimes are firmed up.
326	pub(crate) fn obj_type_name(state: &mut LuaState, o: &LuaValue) -> Result<Vec<u8>, LuaError> {
327	    // C: Table *mt;
328	    // C: if ((ttistable(o) && (mt = hvalue(o)->metatable) != NULL) ||
329	    //        (ttisfulluserdata(o) && (mt = uvalue(o)->metatable) != NULL)) {
330	    //
331	    // TODO(port): same GcRef accessor TBD as in get_tm_by_obj.
332	    let mt: Option<GcRef<lua_types::value::LuaTable>> = match o {
333	        LuaValue::Table(t) => t.metatable(),
334	        LuaValue::UserData(u) => u.metatable(),
335	        _ => None,
336	    };
337	
338	    if let Some(mt_ref) = mt {
339	        // C: const TValue *name = luaH_getshortstr(mt, luaS_new(L, "__name"));
340	        // TODO(port): intern_str can fail with LuaError::Memory; propagating
341	        // here means obj_type_name is now fallible (it wasn't in C, because
342	        // luaS_new's OOM longjmp'd). The caller must use `?`.
343	        let name_key = state.intern_str(b"__name")?;
344	        let name_val = mt_ref.get_short_str(&name_key);
345	        // C: if (ttisstring(name))  /* is '__name' a string? */
346	        //      return getstr(tsvalue(name));  /* use it as type name */
347	        if let LuaValue::Str(s) = name_val {
348	            // C: getstr(tsvalue(name))  →  ts.as_bytes()  →  &[u8]
349	            return Ok(s.as_bytes().to_vec());
350	        }
351	    }
352	
353	    // C: return ttypename(ttype(o));  /* else use standard type name */
354	    Ok(type_name(o.base_type()).to_vec())
355	}
356	
357	// ── luaT_callTM ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
358	
359	// C: void luaT_callTM (lua_State *L, const TValue *f, const TValue *p1,
360	//                      const TValue *p2, const TValue *p3)
361	/// Call tag method `f` with three arguments, discarding any return values.
362	///
363	/// Used for metamethods like `__gc` and `__close` that take three operands
364	/// and whose return value is irrelevant.
365	///
366	/// If the current call frame is Lua bytecode (`isLuacode`), the metamethod
367	/// may yield; otherwise yielding is suppressed (`callnoyield`).
368	pub(crate) fn call_tm(
369	    state: &mut LuaState,

1	// C: lapi.c — Lua public C API
2	// C: $Id: lapi.c $
3	// C: See Copyright Notice in lua.h
4	//
5	// PORT NOTE: This is the Rust-native translation of lapi.c.
6	// The C-API surface (lua_State *, int stack-index protocol) is replaced by
7	// methods on LuaState.  `lua_lock` / `lua_unlock` are dropped (no-op in the
8	// single-threaded default build).  `api_incr_top` is dropped; `state.push()`
9	// already increments.  Stack pointers (StkId) become StackIdx (u32).
10	
11	#![allow(dead_code)]
12	
13	use std::convert::Infallible;
14	#[allow(unused_imports)] use crate::prelude::*;
15	
16	use crate::state::{LuaState, LuaCFunction, GlobalState, CallInfo, CallInfoIdx, StackIdx,
17	    LuaValueExt, LuaTypeExt, StackIdxExt,
18	    LuaTableRefExt, LuaUserDataRefExt, LuaStringRefExt,
19	    LuaLClosureRefExt, LuaClosureExt, LuaProtoExt};
20	use lua_types::{
21	    LuaValue, LuaType, LuaError, LuaString, LuaUserData, LuaClosure, UpVal,
22	    GcRef, LuaStatus,
23	};
24	use lua_types::value::LuaTable;
25	
26	// C: const char lua_ident[] = "$LuaVersion: " LUA_COPYRIGHT " $" ...
27	pub const LUA_IDENT: &[u8] =
28	    b"$LuaVersion: Lua 5.4.7  Copyright (C) 1994-2024 Lua.org, PUC-Rio $\
29	      $LuaAuthors: R. Ierusalimschy, L. H. Figueiredo, W. Celes $";
30	
31	// C: #define LUA_REGISTRYINDEX  (-LUAI_MAXSTACK - 1000)
32	const LUA_REGISTRYINDEX: i32 = -(1_000_000) - 1000;
33	
34	// C: #define LUA_MULTRET  (-1)
35	const LUA_MULTRET: i32 = -1;
36	
37	// C: #define LUA_RIDX_GLOBALS  2
38	const LUA_RIDX_GLOBALS: i64 = 2;
39	
40	// C: #define MAXUPVAL  255
41	const MAX_UPVAL: u8 = 255;
42	
43	// C: #define ispseudo(i)  ((i) <= LUA_REGISTRYINDEX)
44	#[inline]
45	fn is_pseudo(idx: i32) -> bool {
46	    idx <= LUA_REGISTRYINDEX
47	}
48	
49	// C: #define isupvalue(i)  ((i) < LUA_REGISTRYINDEX)
50	#[inline]
51	fn is_upvalue(idx: i32) -> bool {
52	    idx < LUA_REGISTRYINDEX
53	}
54	
55	// C: #define isvalid(L, o)  (!ttisnil(o) || o != &G(L)->nilvalue)
56	// PORT NOTE: In Rust there is no nilvalue pointer sentinel; a value is valid
57	// when it is a real stack/upvalue slot (not the canonical nil placeholder).
58	// We encode "invalid" as a special sentinel — here a unit Option.
59	#[inline]
60	fn is_valid(val: &LuaValue) -> bool {
61	    // In C, the only "invalid" TValue is the global nilvalue singleton.
62	    // In Rust the registry pseudo-index returns LuaValue::Nil when the index
63	    // is out of range; callers that must distinguish this use is_valid_slot.
64	    // For Phase A we treat all values as valid; the subtlety only matters for
65	    // pseudo-index writes.
66	    // TODO(port): distinguish G(L)->nilvalue sentinel from ordinary Nil for
67	    // exact parity with C's isvalid() check. For now, Nil returned from an
68	    // out-of-range index is treated as invalid.
69	    !matches!(val, LuaValue::Nil)
70	}
71	
72	// ── index helpers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
73	
74	// C: static TValue *index2value (lua_State *L, int idx)
75	// PORT NOTE: In Rust we cannot return a pointer; we return a cloned LuaValue.
76	// Writers use a companion index_to_stack_idx() for actual stack slots.
77	fn index_to_value(state: &LuaState, idx: i32) -> LuaValue {
78	    // C: CallInfo *ci = L->ci;
79	    let ci = state.current_call_info();
80	    if idx > 0 {

+    /// the stack, and return a copy of its bytes. Mirrors `luaL_tolstring`

+    /// the metamethod result over the default formatting below.

content.text:
Build is green.

**Report:**

- **Modified:** `crates/lua-vm/src/api.rs` — added inherent `LuaState::to_display_string` (43 new lines after `push_value_at` near line 346).
- **What it does:** Faithful port of `luaL_tolstring` (lauxlib.c:898). Pushes a display-string copy of the value at `idx` onto the stack and also returns its bytes. Handles primitives directly: strings duplicate, numbers use `num_to_string` (`%I`/`%.14g`), booleans → `"true"`/`"false"`, nil → `"nil"`. Reference types format as `"<typename>: 0x<hex>"` via `tagmethods::obj_type_name` (which honors `__name`) and `to_pointer`.
- **Resolution:** Inherent method wins over the `LuaStateStubExt::to_display_string` trait default per the Phase-B-reconcile pattern — no edit to `state_stub.rs` needed.
- **TODOs surfacing next:** A `TODO(port)` flags that `__tostring` metamethod dispatch is not yet wired (needs `luaL_callmeta`/`get_metafield` analogues). For tables/functions/threads/userdata the inner `obj_type_name` call may itself hit `intern_str` paths; those are implemented. `write_output` (called by the `print_fn` caller) is still a `todo!()` and will likely be the next iteration's target.
- **`cargo build -p lua-cli`:** green (warnings only).

result:
Build is green.

**Report:**

- **Modified:** `crates/lua-vm/src/api.rs` — added inherent `LuaState::to_display_string` (43 new lines after `push_value_at` near line 346).
- **What it does:** Faithful port of `luaL_tolstring` (lauxlib.c:898). Pushes a display-string copy of the value at `idx` onto the stack and also returns its bytes. Handles primitives directly: strings duplicate, numbers use `num_to_string` (`%I`/`%.14g`), booleans → `"true"`/`"false"`, nil → `"nil"`. Reference types format as `"<typename>: 0x<hex>"` via `tagmethods::obj_type_name` (which honors `__name`) and `to_pointer`.
- **Resolution:** Inherent method wins over the `LuaStateStubExt::to_display_string` trait default per the Phase-B-reconcile pattern — no edit to `state_stub.rs` needed.
- **TODOs surfacing next:** A `TODO(port)` flags that `__tostring` metamethod dispatch is not yet wired (needs `luaL_callmeta`/`get_metafield` analogues). For tables/functions/threads/userdata the inner `obj_type_name` call may itself hit `intern_str` paths; those are implemented. `write_output` (called by the `print_fn` caller) is still a `todo!()` and will likely be the next iteration's target.
- **`cargo build -p lua-cli`:** green (warnings only).
